meta.title: AFTER score — epilepsy after de novo status epilepticus
meta.authors: Rodrigo-Gisbert M, Toledo M, Santamarina E, et al.
meta.outcome: Risk of developing epilepsy (a first unprovoked/remote seizure meeting ILAE criteria) within 5 years after a de novo episode of status epilepticus, in adults with no prior epilepsy.
meta.source_note: AFTER score: Rodrigo-Gisbert et al., Epilepsy Behav 2023 (doi 10.1016/j.yebeh.2023.109531, PMID 37995538). Derived by LASSO-Cox in 230 adults with de novo SE (23.9% developed epilepsy); 5-year cumulative risk by Kaplan-Meier; time-dependent AUC 0.743. Not yet externally validated.
meta.cff_info:
What this is. The AFTER score estimates the risk of developing epilepsy (a later unprovoked seizure) in the 5 years after a first (de novo) episode of status epilepticus, in a patient with no prior epilepsy. It is the only published points score for this question and complements the group's mortality/functional-outcome tools.

How it is scored. Four items, 1 point each (total 0–4): Activity pattern in EEG (rhythmic/periodic patterns — LRDA/LPD/GPD), First-line Treatment delayed ≥1 hour, cryptogenic Etiology, and super-Refractoriness.

What it means. The validated cut-off is ≥2: patients scoring ≥2 had a 5-year epilepsy risk of ≈66% versus ≈23% for a score of 0–1 (p<0.0001; AUC 0.74). Risk rises further with each point (Fig 3).

Caveats. Single-centre (Vall d'Hebron), de novo SE only (acute-symptomatic or cryptogenic; not prior epilepsy), not yet externally validated. 5-year figures are Kaplan-Meier estimates (median follow-up ~3 years), so they extrapolate beyond most patients' follow-up. Population-level estimate — apply clinical judgement.

source_link.label: PubMed
source_link.title: Rodrigo-Gisbert et al., Epilepsy Behav 2023 — AFTER score

predictors.name: Rhythmic / periodic EEG pattern
predictors.assess: Rhythmic or periodic patterns (RPPs) on EEG — lateralized rhythmic delta activity (LRDA), lateralized periodic discharges (LPDs), or generalized periodic discharges (GPDs). Present = 1 point.

predictors.name: First-line treatment delayed ≥ 1 hour
predictors.assess: Time from SE onset to first-line (benzodiazepine) treatment of 1 hour or more. Delayed ≥1 h = 1 point.

predictors.name: Cryptogenic aetiology
predictors.assess: Aetiology of the SE. Cryptogenic (no cause found) = 1 point; acute symptomatic = 0. (This model covers de novo SE — acute-symptomatic or cryptogenic — not remote/progressive causes.)

predictors.name: Super-refractory SE
predictors.assess: Super-refractory status epilepticus — SE continuing or recurring ≥24 h after starting anaesthesia, including on weaning. Present = 1 point.

model.panel_title: AFTER — epilepsy risk after de novo status epilepticus

rows.label: AFTER 3 — above cut-off (≥2): ~66% 5-year epilepsy risk (rising with score)
AFTER 4 — above cut-off (≥2): ~66% 5-year epilepsy risk (highest band)

model.note: 5-year risk of post-SE epilepsy by the AFTER score (Rodrigo-Gisbert 2023). Validated as a dichotomy at the cut-off ≥2: 0–1 → ~23% vs ≥2 → ~66% 5-year Kaplan-Meier risk (p<0.0001; AUC 0.74). Per-score curves (Fig 3) show a further gradient within the ≥2 band but with small numbers (score 3 n=20, score 4 n=2), so rows use the validated two-band risks rather than noisy per-point values.
model.viz_info: Each item — rhythmic/periodic EEG pattern, delayed first-line treatment, cryptogenic aetiology, super-refractoriness — adds a point (waterfall). A total of 0–1 carries ~23% 5-year risk of developing epilepsy; ≥2 (the validated threshold) carries ~66%. The donut shows the band's 5-year epilepsy risk.

provenance.source: AFTER point items taken verbatim from Rodrigo-Gisbert et al., Epilepsy Behav 2023, Table 3 (Activity pattern in EEG [RPPs] 0/1; First-line Treatment ≥1 h 0/1; cryptogenic Etiology 0/1; super-Refractoriness 0/1; total 0–4). Dichotomy risks from Fig 4 (AFTER <2 → 23.0% vs ≥2 → 66.2% 5-year KM risk); per-score distribution + curves from Fig 3. PDF in 0_inbox/papers/status_epilepticus/.
provenance.notes: Score kind, 4 binary predictors, total 0–4. Outcome = 5-year KM risk of developing epilepsy after de novo SE. Validated cut-off ≥2 (66% vs 23%); rows carry the two-band risks (0–1 → 23%, 2–4 → 66%) because per-score n is small (15/93/100/20/2). Fills the site's post-SE-epilepsy gap; adults, de novo SE only. Not externally validated (single-centre).
